title: UDLM Provider — Adopted Standard Support
description: The provider-capability fragment by which a provider DECLARES which versions of each adopted external standard it can emit and/or consume (adopted-standards.md §3.2; DCM ADS-001). This is DATA — DCM negotiates the consumer's required version against this matrix (ADS-003), and the schema deliberately does NOT interpret ranges (resolution is Policy, T2). A full provider registration includes this fragment; this schema validates the fragment.

kind.description: Provider type (provider-contract.md). A provider may emit adopted-standard data in its Information-Provider role even when it also provisions (service).

adopted_standard_support.description: One entry per adopted standard this provider supports.

standard.description: External standard name, e.g. FOCUS, OpenCost, OSCAL, SCIM.

supports.description: The version range/pins the provider can serve — a pin ("1.4"), wildcard ("1.x"), or range (">=1.2 <2.0"). RANGE RESOLUTION is Policy (DCM ADS-003), not the schema.

preferred.description: The concrete version the provider prefers within `supports` (DCM binds here when it falls inside the negotiated overlap).

direction.description: Whether the provider produces the standard's data, consumes it, or both.
